what_it_does: Batch ingestion script that reads a scattered project corpus, classifies each file by form, extracts static metadata (frontmatter, links, code blocks) via lift, and invokes a local LLM to produce either a cheap one-line summary (light files) or a structured interpretation (principle, framing, reach, surface claims, form-specific fields) for substantive files. It then writes the resulting records, links, and blocks into a ledger database keyed by a run id. The __main__ block supports a stratified --sample mode across form buckets.
observations: Imports companion modules db, lift, fleet, forms and uses a hardcoded ROOT path.
survey_forms() reads a JSONL form survey and builds a rel->content_form map, skipping lines with errors or parse failures.
capture_one() uses lift.lift(path) to obtain frontmatter, links, and blocks, then forms.bucket() to decide light vs substance capture.
Light files get only a one-line summary from fleet.local4b after reading 4000 characters.
Substantive files read up to 110000 characters and receive a detailed structured-extraction prompt that explicitly treats surface claims as unverified evidence.
The prompt asks for strict JSON matching a schema returned by forms.schema_for(bk).
write() persists files, links, and blocks via db helpers and logs the run.
__main__ loads carve.json, builds reps, and optionally draws a stratified sample of up to 3 files per bucket before capturing with 6 workers.
standouts: Two-tier capture strategy (light vs substance) driven by form classification.
Explicit epistemic framing: AI-generated corpus is untrustworthy at detail level; surface claims are evidence, not fact; the 'gold' is the author's underlying principle/intent.
Stratified sampling utility in __main__ for quick validation runs.
Combines static code lifting with LLM interpretation and graph-style persistence (files, links, blocks).
conventions: Uses module-level globals (ROOT, FORMS) initialized at import time.
Relies on companion modules for database, lifting, model fleet, and form taxonomy.
JSON parsing uses bare except/pass patterns.
Hardcodes local filesystem paths and token/character budgets.
concerns: Hardcoded ROOT and survey path make the script environment-specific.
Broad exception handling may swallow parse and I/O errors silently.
No validation that the LLM response conforms to the requested schema beyond JSON loading.
Reading exactly 110000 characters can cut mid-token or mid-structure for large files.
write() opens a new DB connection per call rather than accepting an injected connection.
notes: A concrete ingestion worker that turns raw files into interpreted ledger records, with a deliberate light/heavy capture split and an unusual 'distrust the surface' prompt philosophy.
questions: What exact rules does forms.bucket() use to mark a file as light?
What JSON schema does forms.schema_for(bk) produce for each form bucket?
What database schema do db.upsert_file, db.add_link, and db.add_block expect?
How does lift.lift() identify links and blocks, and what does has_fm indicate?
What is the structure of carve.json and what do cluster tags represent?
Why is the substantive body limit set to 110000 characters?
purpose_vs_actual: The file does what it appears designed to do: capture per-file interpretive metadata from an AI-generated corpus and persist it into a ledger, plus provide a stratified sampling mode for testing.
apparent_themes: Extraction of author mental models over literal claims
Two-speed processing based on content classification
intention_signals: The script is trying to bootstrap a semantic inventory of a messy, AI-generated codebase by separating trustworthy structural signals (frontmatter, links, blocks) from interpretive claims, and by cheaply summarizing low-stakes files while deeply analyzing high-stakes ones.
novelty: The explicit prompt-level instruction to treat the corpus as 'SCATTERED, AI-GENERATED, NEVER-REVIEWED' and to value underlying principle over surface claims, combined with a form-driven light/substance capture split.
contribution: Provides an end-to-end capture pipeline that fuses static extraction, form classification, tiered LLM interpretation, and ledger persistence, producing records that distinguish structural facts from inferred claims.
summary_for_embedding: capture.py is a batch ingestion script that extracts static file metadata and LLM-derived interpretive summaries from a scattered AI-generated project corpus and writes them into a ledger database.
intention_for_embedding: To populate a structured ledger with per-file mental-model interpretations while separating verified structure from unverified surface claims.

symbols.does: Loads the form survey JSONL and returns a rel-to-content_form map, skipping error records and parse failures.
symbols.description: survey_forms() reads ~/wizard-run-1/form_survey.jsonl line by line, parses each JSON object, ignores lines containing an 'error' field or that fail to parse, and returns a dictionary mapping each rel value to its content_form string.

symbols.does: Builds a single file's interpretive record by lifting metadata, classifying the form, and invoking a local LLM for either a light summary or a structured interpretation.
symbols.description: capture_one(rel) constructs a record by calling lift.lift() for frontmatter, links, and blocks, using forms.bucket() to choose a light or substance path, reading a bounded amount of file content, prompting fleet.local4b() for either a one-line summary or a strict JSON interpretation (principle, framing, reach, surface_claims, form_specific), and packaging the result with links and blocks.

symbols.does: Persists a list of capture records and their associated links and blocks into the database under a run id.
symbols.description: write(recs, run_id) opens a database connection, logs the run, upserts each file record with interpretive fields via db.upsert_file(), inserts all outgoing links via db.add_link(), inserts all code blocks via db.add_block(), then commits and closes the connection.
